steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Command of Evidence — Quantitative question. The city planner concludes that ads aimed at the general public should emphasize medicinal plants, so the correct choice must show that the general public values medicinal plants more than the other listed services (i.e., medicinal plants has the best — lowest — ranking in the general public column).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The prevention of soil erosion was ranked higher for the general public than was the provision of medicinal plants.’: Contradicts the planner’s reasoning. If soil erosion outranked medicinal plants for the general public, the planner should have emphasized soil erosion, not medicinal plants. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The provision of medicinal plants was ranked higher for the general public than were the other four services.’: Directly supports the planner’s conclusion. If medicinal plants has the highest ranking (lowest number) among all five services for the general public, it is exactly the service the planner should feature in ads. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘The provision of medicinal plants was ranked higher for stakeholders than it was for the general public.’: Compares two different audiences rather than showing what the general public values most; it does not establish that medicinal plants tops the general public’s list. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The enhancement of pollination was ranked lower for the general public than it was for project leaders.’: Concerns pollination, not medicinal plants, and compares the general public to project leaders rather than identifying what the general public values most. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
